game_name: Super Mario RPG: Legend of the Seven Stars

design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based JRPG combat with timed action commands integrated into isometric 3D platforming exploration. Party management (selecting 3 of 5 characters) drives tactical decision-making across non-linear regional progression.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing offensive specialization (Geno, Bowser) vs. support capability (Peach's healing) within limited party slots; action command timing for damage optimization creates skill-based combat resolution.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience Mario characters in a deep narrative RPG adventure with strategic party composition, while maintaining Super Mario's exploration and platforming identity in isometric 3D spaces. Rescue Peach while discovering new allies (Mallow, Geno) and recruiting former antagonist Bowser.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid that privileges narrative depth and character development (Final Fantasy influence via Square) while insisting Mario platforming remains central to exploration and world traversal. No random encounters—all enemies visible, allowing skilled players to avoid battles. Action commands create skill-expressive turn-based combat (timed button presses amplify effectiveness), rejecting pure RNG-dependent JRPG resolution.

difficulty.checkpointing: Regional progression with save points; battle defeat results in loss of coins rather than hard game-over, reducing friction
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: visible enemies enable players to select/avoid battles strategically. Action command timing allows players to execute attacks with precision for enhanced damage. Party composition (swappable between up to 5 characters) allows tactical adaptation to enemy weaknesses.

progression.upgrade_system: Character-driven: each of five party members (Mario, Peach, Bowser, Mallow, Geno) has unique attack/magic move sets that scale through leveling. Special techniques unlock naturally via story progression and character acquisition.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated character recruitment (Mallow early, Bowser mid-game, Geno, Peach rejoins post-separation). Geographically diverse regions (Mushroom Kingdom, Moleville, Monstro Town, Yo'ster Isle, Nimbus Land, Smithy Gang's homeworld) unlock sequentially as plot demands. Star Piece collection gates access to final area.
replayability_hooks: Five distinct characters with unique move pools encourage alternate party compositions on replays
Action command timing mastery rewards repeated playthroughs for optimized combat
Hidden mechanics (enemy avoidance, item collection) discoverable through exploration

world_building_techniques: Geographically-distinct regions with unique inhabitant cultures (Toads, moles, cloud people, Yoshis, reformed monsters in Monstro Town)
Character-driven narrative: Mallow's identity crisis (thinks he's a tadpole), Geno's celestial origin, Bowser's redemption arc via desertion and forced alliance
Environmental storytelling through castle ruins, regional architecture reflecting inhabitant species
Dialogue-heavy exposition introducing each region's problems and inhabitants' concerns

boss_design.philosophy: Signature antagonist encounters (Exor, Smithy Gang lieutenants) framed as dramatic story beats. Bosses represent obstacles to Star Road restoration; defeat is narrative progression, not mechanical punishment.
notable_bosses: Exor (giant animate sword, initial inciting incident—breaks Star Road)
Claymorton/Mack (Smithy Gang lieutenant, first major challenge in Mushroom Kingdom)
Smithy (final boss, robotic blacksmith leader from alternate world with world domination aspirations)

faction_indicators: Smithy Gang: invading force from parallel world, mechanically-themed (robotic leadership), displacing native factions
Mario's party: alliance of former enemies (Bowser), celestial agents (Geno), cloud natives (Mallow), and royalty (Peach)
Reformed monsters in Monstro Town: suggests post-conflict societal integration possibility
danger_signals: Smithy Gang's arrival scatters Star Road fragments across world, fragmenting wish-granting infrastructure
Exor's sword form and destructive entry signal violent external invasion rather than internal conflict
Bowser's army desertion due to fear indicates psychological dominance and authority shift
environmental_storytelling: Destroyed bridges and landscape damage from Smithy Gang incursion visible in progression
Regional character (mole mines, cloud platforms, Yoshi island) reflects native culture preservation despite invasion
Bowser's Castle serves as both antagonist stronghold and later gateway/sanctuary, showing environmental recontextualization
